name: Gmail
description: Gmail plugin for OpenClaw. IMAP for reading/searching (X-GM-RAW, multi-term AND, hasAttachment, thread fetch, HTML→text fallback, attachment download) and Gmail API for sending/reply.

tools.name: gmail_authorize
tools.description: Start Gmail OAuth authorization flow. Opens a browser window, waits for the user to complete authorization, exchanges the code for tokens, saves them, and returns the authorized email. This call blocks until the browser flow completes (user grants or denies access). Call this when any gmail tool returns an authorization error.
tools.searchHint: gmail authorize oauth login auth permission grant access

forceRelogin.description: If true, skip the already authorized check and force re-authorization

tools.name: gmail_mailboxes_list
tools.description: List the mailboxes (folders/labels) on the configured Gmail account.
tools.searchHint: gmail mailboxes folders labels list inbox sent trash

tools.name: gmail_messages_search
tools.description: Server-side IMAP search. `query` accepts free text (AND by default) or `OR` for alternation. Inline Gmail-style operators are auto-extracted: `from:`, `to:`, `subject:`, `has:attachment`, `is:unread`, `is:starred`, `in:LABEL`, `before:YYYY-MM-DD`, `after:YYYY-MM-DD`. Explicit params win over inline operators. Use `gmailRaw` to pass a raw Gmail search expression (Gmail only). When no date range is provided, defaults to the last 30 days.
tools.searchHint: gmail search messages email inbox find filter query

query.description: Free-text search terms. Multi-term is AND; use OR for alternation. Supports inline Gmail operators.

from.description: Filter by sender email or name

since.description: Messages after this date (ISO 8601 or YYYY-MM-DD)

before.description: Messages before this date. Use the date field from a previous result for pagination.

gmailRaw.description: Raw Gmail search expression (X-GM-RAW, Gmail only). Overrides all other filters.

tools.name: gmail_message_get
tools.description: Fetch one message by UID with full body text and attachment metadata. HTML-only messages are auto-converted to plain text. Returns body, headers, attachments list, and thread info.
tools.searchHint: gmail get message fetch read email detail body uid

tools.name: gmail_thread_get
tools.description: Fetch all messages in the same Gmail thread as the given UID, ordered chronologically. Requires Gmail (X-GM-EXT-1 IMAP extension). All thread messages are fetched in a single IMAP round-trip.
tools.searchHint: gmail thread conversation messages get fetch

uid.description: Any message UID in the thread

tools.name: gmail_message_attachments_save
tools.description: Download all (or filtered) attachments from one message to disk. Returns absolute file paths for chaining with other tools like Read.

filenames.description: Specific filenames to download. Omit to download all.

tools.name: gmail_message_update
tools.description: Mark one message as read/unread and/or set/clear its starred (flagged) state. At least one flag update is required.
tools.searchHint: gmail mark read unread star flag update

tools.name: gmail_message_move
tools.description: Move one message to another mailbox/folder.

tools.name: gmail_message_send
tools.description: Send a new email via Gmail API with OAuth 2.0. Requires `confirm: true` as a safety guardrail (unless requireExplicitSendConfirmation is disabled). Recipients can be a single string or an array. Attachments support local file paths.
tools.searchHint: gmail send email message compose new mail

path.description: Absolute path to the file to attach

confirm.description: Must be true to actually send. Safety guardrail against accidental sends.

tools.name: gmail_message_reply
tools.description: Reply to an existing message by UID. Automatically sets In-Reply-To and References headers for proper threading. Quoted original text is appended. Set replyAll=true to CC all original recipients (excluding self). Requires `confirm: true`.
tools.searchHint: gmail reply email respond answer thread

mailbox.description: Mailbox containing the original message (default: INBOX)

uid.description: UID of the message to reply to

confirm.description: Must be true to actually send.

username.description: Full email address used as IMAP username and sender identity

appPassword.description: Gmail App Password (16 characters, no spaces). Only needed for IMAP fallback auth. Generate at https://myaccount.google.com/apppasswords

accessToken.description: Google OAuth 2.0 access token for IMAP and Gmail API. Auto-managed by TelyClaw — do not set manually.

attachmentsDir.description: Directory where attachments are saved. Defaults to ~/.openclaw/inbox/gmail.

requireExplicitSendConfirmation.description: When true, send and reply tools require confirm=true.

requireExplicitSendConfirmation.label: Require send confirmation
requireExplicitSendConfirmation.help: Block accidental sends. When enabled the agent must pass confirm=true to send/reply.
